title: ModelManual authority module (typed Rust structs + MANUAL_VERSION)
goal: Land src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_manual/mod.rs as the single source-of-truth for ModelManual content per spec §10.15.1 (AC-MODEL-MANUAL-AUTHORITY). Typed Rust structs (ManualCommand, ManualWorkflow, ManualSafetyConstraint, ManualFeatureGroup, CommandReference) carry the content; MANUAL_VERSION is a `pub const &str` bumped on every wired-surface change.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_manual/{mod.rs,types.rs,content.rs}; types.rs defines the struct hierarchy; content.rs holds the v1 manual content as Rust const data; mod.rs exposes the public API.
unblock_conditions: No prerequisites; this is the foundation MT for A.6.
implementation_notes: Per spec §10.15.1 there is NO separate MODEL_MANUAL.md file that competes for authority; the typed Rust structs ARE the manual. Struct hierarchy: `Manual { version: &'static str, feature_groups: &'static [ManualFeatureGroup], command_reference: &'static [CommandReference], safety_constraints: &'static [ManualSafetyConstraint], workflows: &'static [ManualWorkflow] }`. ManualFeatureGroup `{ id, title, description, commands: &'static [&'static str] (refs into command_reference) }`. CommandReference `{ id, name, ipc_channel: Option<&'static str>, tauri_command: Option<&'static str>, schema_fields: &'static [&'static str], cli_flag: Option<&'static str>, description, expected_input, expected_output, common_errors: &'static [&'static str], recovery_steps: &'static [&'static str] }`. ManualWorkflow `{ id, title, prerequisites, steps: &'static [&'static str], expected_outcome, failure_modes: &'static [&'static str] }`. ManualSafetyConstraint `{ id, constraint_text, enforcement_point }`. MANUAL_VERSION starts at "1.0.0". The v1 content covers: (a) feature_groups for Sandbox (Cluster B), ModelRuntime (Cluster C), Memory/SelfImprovement (Cluster D), HBR/ProcessLedger/Diagnostics (Cluster A), (b) command_reference for every Tauri IPC channel KERNEL-004 ships (placeholders for cluster B/C/D commands marked `status: "planned"` until those MTs land — but the A-cluster channels declared by MT-013 IPC bridge, MT-027 visual debug IPC, MT-030 inspector IPC, MT-036 swarm harness CLI must all be present at v1.0.0), (c) safety_constraints citing HBR rules, (d) workflows covering startup, basic governed-session run, and diagnostics-panel triage. Implement as compile-time const data so the manual is a single immutable snapshot per build. Test: assert MANUAL_VERSION matches a SemVer pattern; assert every CommandReference id is unique; assert every ManualFeatureGroup.commands ref resolves to a CommandReference; assert no orphan CommandReference exists.
risk_if_missed: Without typed authority, prose .md drift becomes the de facto manual and HBR-MAN-003 self-consistency cannot be enforced mechanically.
validator_focus: Manual is pure const data (no I/O, no allocation); ids are unique; refs resolve; MANUAL_VERSION is a public const visible from outside the crate.

minimum_controls: MANUAL_VERSION is `pub const` so HBR-MAN-001 paired-diff check can grep for it from the .GOV scripts.
Content is const (no runtime mutation) so the manual is a build-time artifact only.
